meaning: adj.无活动的,惰性的,迟钝的
examples: Inert gases like neon and argon have eight electrons in their outer shell.
Agronomists studying the herb have discovered that it produces large amounts of histidine, an amino acid that, in test-tube solutions, renders these metals chemically inert.
The novel itself remains oddly inert.
Not all of the placebo treatments that the researchers looked at in this study are inert.
Investigators made sure the materials in what appeared to be a bomb were inert.
